title: Four Against Darkness - Deep Dive Part 8 - The Knight of Destiny
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M61DCig7ZJY

Daniel (@dungeondive) introduces 9D6 Quest, a standalone dice management game by the creator of Four Against Darkness. Unlike the dungeon-crawling format of the main game, 9D6 Quest tasks you with completing nine sequential missions across randomized hex maps, managing a pool of nine six-sided dice that degrade as you take damage. Your chosen character class provides special abilities—though fair warning, the Ranger is arguably overpowered with its difficulty-reduction mechanic.

The game delivers excellent press-your-luck tension as you decide how many dice to risk on each test, knowing that rolling ones downgrades your dice from fresh to tired to wounded to dead. With six character classes, varied mission types, multiple magical items, and boss encounters that scale with your party, there's surprising depth beneath the light, portable package. While originally available as a physical book, getting the PDF allows easy printing of character sheets and mission tracking.

----

Have you picked up 9D6 Quest, and if so, which character class did you choose? Does the dice management mechanic appeal to you as a core gameplay loop?
